english: seized upon their territorial shares Glr.; bla-srid (bla srid) Cs. a Lama's dominion. — sde-srid (sde srid) province; čos-srid (chos srid) clerical government, ecclesiastical dominion. — 3. ruler, commander, regent, reigning prince; so also in the compounds just mentioned.

I. vb., 1. to be, to exist (?). — 2. to be possible, often preceded by yań (yang); skyé-ba dań °jig-pa kún-la sríd-na (skye ba dang hjig pa kun la srid na) since springing up and passing away is the lot of all men Dzl.; pʽán-pa žig srid (phan pa zhig srid) healing is possible Pth.; °dí-las sla yań srid (hdi las sla yang srid) it might be easier (for me) then than now Dzl.; de-bžin-du bden srid snyám-nas (de bzhin du bden srid snyam nas) thinking this might possibly be true; the verb is usually put in the infinitive mood terminating in pa (pa): de yín-pa-ań srid (de yin pa ang srid) after all it might be this man, it might be he Mil.; °dír °óń-ba mi srid-do (hdir hong ba mi srid do), bód-du brós-pa srid (bod du bros pa srid) he will scarcely come back, he will have escaped to Tibet Glr.; sometimes with the root of the verb: yoń mi srid (yong mi srid) Mil.; bdag tʽar kyań sríd-kyis (bdag thar kyang srid kyis) as it is a possible case, that we might be released Dzl.; ma srid čig (ma srid cig) about the same as: God forbid! by no means! In W. nearly = to be obliged: *kyer-wa-la srid* (*kyer wa la srid*) now it will be my lot, now I shall be obliged, to carry (twice as much), *sád-če-la srid* (*sad ce la srid*) (B.: bab (bab) or tʽug (thug)) he deserves death, he must die. II. sbst. भवन 1. existence, state of being, life, sríd-pa gžan nyáms-su myóń-ba (srid pa gzhan nyams su myong ba) to experience, to pass through, other periods of existence Wdn., srid pʽyi-ma (srid phyi ma) Sch.: the future period of life, of existence. — 2. things existing, the world, sríd(-pa) gsum (srid (-pa) gsum) the three worlds, sríd-pai °kʽór-lo (srid pai hkhor lo) Cs.: the revolving system (the world's cycle); sríd-pai mtsʽo (srid pai mtsho) the ocean of existence, sríd-pai čʽu-klúń čʽén-po (srid pai chu klung chen po) the stream of existence Mil.; also a single being, commonly however srid-pa-pa (srid pa pa); bár-doi srid-pa (bar doi srid pa), bár-srid-pa (bar srid pa) Thgy., bár-ma-doi srid-pa-pa (bar ma doi srid pa pa) Sty. the beings in the Bardo, v. bár-do (bar do). — The meaning of srid (srid) in srid-pai bar-do (srid pai bar do), and in some other expressions, have yet to be determined. — 3. symb. num.: 14.
Lt.? W. *srin-gláń-čan* (*srin glang can*) having the staggers (of horses); being mad.

Ssk. राक्षस, fem. srin-mo (srin mo), demons, figuring in Indian and Tibetan mythology. They are supposed to be, for the most part, of an enormous size, generally hostile to mankind, going about at night, to ensnare and even to devour human beings. Their chief abode was Ceylon, and also Tibet was originally inhabited by them. The Tibetans are even said to be the descendants of an ape (sent by, or emanated from, Avalokiteshvara) and of a Tibetan Srinmo (srin mo). brág-srin (brag srin) rock-Srinpo (srin po) or Srinmo (srin mo); °dre-srin (hdre srin) goblins and Srinpos (srin po s); čʽu-srin (chu srin) v. the following article.

= bu (bu), insect, worm, vermin; srin-bu pád-ma (srin bu pad ma) (srin-pa (srin pa) Sik.) leech, srin-bu me-kʽyér (srin bu me khyer) glow-worm; rgyu-srin (rgyu srin), koń-srin (kong srin) intestinal worm; pʽyi-srin (phyi srin) vermin living on the skin Lt.; dár-srin (dar srin) silk-worm; srin-bál (srin bal) acc. to Wdn. = rás-bál (ras bal) cotton, Sch., Schr.: flock-silk; raw silk; srin-byá (srin bya) nocturnal bird, owl etc. Lt.; srin-tʽór (srin thor) small ulcer or tumour; srin-šiń (srin shing) Med., Sch.: mulberry-tree; čʽu-srin (chu srin) a monster living in the water.
1. darkness, gloom, night. — 2. shady side, north side of a mountain. — srib-pa (srib pa) vb., to grow dark or dusky, C.: *sa srib soń* (*sa srib song*) night has begun.
Glr., srú-mo (sru mo) Lex. and C. mother's sister, aunt.
W. for sprúg-pa (sprug pa), srúb-pa (srub pa) and dkrúg-pa (dkrug pa): 1. to shake, to shake out. — 2. to stir, stir up, twirl. — 3. to shake, to make to totter.

I. vb., pf. (b)sruńs ((b)srungs), fut. bsruń (bsrung), imp. (b)sruń(s) ((b)srung(s)), Ssk. रक्ष्, 1. to watch, to keep guard intrs.; but gen. trans., i.e. to watch, to keep, to guard, to keep in custody, kʽyím (khyim) the house Dzl.; to save from, to pro-
